value.Title: SWINERTON INCORPORATED - HIGHLIGHTS FROM AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. AND PEER COMPANIES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGB-XRV3-RRRH-604K-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - HIGHLIGHTS FROM AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. AND PEER COMPANIES2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:29ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - HIGHLIGHTS FROM AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. AND PEER COMPANIES

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 Press Releases]

+ SWINERTON TAPS PROVEN DUO TO DRIVE NATIONAL ACCOUNTS

With over $1 billion in growth and over 250 major projects under their belts, Swinerton's Chris Chany and Derek Jaschke step into new roles to drive client-focused expansion. Swinerton announces two strategic leadership promotions to continue delivering exceptional client experiences nationwide. Effective July 1, Chris Chany has been promoted to lead National Markets, while Derek Jaschke has been named Director of National Accounts, succeeding Chany.

Chany, known for his relationship-driven leadership style, previously served as Director of National Accounts, where he was instrumental in expanding Swinerton's client base and driving growth. Under his leadership, the national accounts sales model grew from $350 million to more than $1 billion in just four years. Chany has strengthened partnerships across multiple markets and sectors, generating repeat business.

"Chris' ability to strategize with teams from pre-sell to pursuit, through project execution and closeout, has allowed Swinerton to grow and solidify critical client relationships and seize growth opportunities for our teams," shares Don Adair, Swinerton's CRO. "Throughout his career, Chris has emphasized relationships, extraordinary client experiences, dependable project delivery and consistently generating repeat business."

Source: Company Website

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED WILLIAM SILVA NAMED DIRECTOR OF NATIONAL MASS TIMBER

Instrumental leader to enhance Swinerton Builders' mass timber capabilities Swinerton, a national commercial general contractor and construction manager with office locations nationwide, continues its commitment to advancing and accelerating the adoption of mass timber construction with the appointment of William Silva as Director, National Mass Timber.

In this position, Silva will lead the creation of a Mass Timber Center of Excellence, a cross-functional initiative designed by Swinerton to drive innovation, collaboration and integrate the company's extensive general contracting expertise with its affiliate firm, Timberlab. Timberlab's specialized capabilities include mass timber procurement, manufacturing, fabrication, engineering and design. The center will serve as a hub for innovation, education, and operational excellence, empowering Swinerton teams nationwide to deliver exceptional mass timber projects and continue to be a trusted resource for its clients, design partners, and engineering partners.

Source: Company Website

+ SWINERTON TOPS-OUT LATEST MASS TIMBER MULTIFAMILY PROJECT IN CHARLOTTE

Swinerton, an industry-leading commercial general contractor with offices nationwide, announces the topping out of Cordo, an $80 million multifamily development in Charlotte's Optimist Park and the construction firm's fifth collaboration with Space Craft. Just blocks from The Joinery and Oxbow, also developed by Space Craft and built by Swinerton, Cordo is designed to be a walkable, transit-friendly community for active families and young professionals. The hybrid-structure broke ground last fall and is expected to be ready for occupancy by summer 2027.

"There is a distinct comfort level when you have a long-term relationship with your construction crew that spans multiple projects," says Brian Nicholson, director of construction, Space Craft. "Cordo is the fifth project we've embarked on with Swinerton, and the team continues to impress us with an unwavering commitment to quality and ability to deliver on our vision. With each community, we set out to build a sustainable, modern neighborhood for today. Swinerton has been instrumental in helping us shape the Mill District into one of Charlotte's hottest neighborhoods."

Situated on a three-acre site in Charlotte's Optimist Park community, Cordo totals seven floors with 3,000 square feet of street-level retail just steps from Cordelia Park, Optimist Hall and LYNX Blue Line light rail access. The 325,000-square foot development features 287studio, one-, two-, three- and four-bedroom units and offers premium amenities including a rooftop terrace, private courtyard, electric bicycles for rent, a fitness center, a shared dining area and coworking and meeting space. A Mass timber brings a biophilic element into each residence with cross-laminated timber (CLT) decks creating exposed timber ceilings. Charlotte architecture firm Shook Kelley designed the community located at 515 Parkwood Ave.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ AUSTIN NAMED NO. 1 SCIENCE + TECHNOLOGY CONSTRUCTION FIRM

DALLAS - Austin is honored to once again be named the No. 1 Science + Technology Facility Construction Firm in the U.S. by World of Construction.

‍

From semiconductor fabs to digital infrastructure, our teams pour their skill, care, and dedication into creating the specialized spaces that help fuel tomorrow's breakthroughs. It's thanks to our employee‑owners, partners, and clients that this work is possible, and we're grateful for the trust they place in us every day.

‍

Firms recognized by World of Construction are those that are leading the way in delivering some of the most advanced science and technology facilities in the world. Austin is grateful for the recognition and proud to be leading our industry.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN EARNS MULTIPLE HONORS AT ASA HOUSTON EVENT

HOUSTON - Austin Commercial was honored to be finalists for multiple Excellence in Construction Awards from American Subcontractors Association-Houston Chapter. Austin's Houston office was awarded the GC Safety Award and the Project of the Year Over $50M for the UH Memorial Hermann Football Operations Center.

‍

The company extends its thanks to our trade partners and employee-owners for all that you do, and for making these honors possible.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN RANKED #27 ON BD+C'S TOP 100 HOSPITAL FACILITY CONSTRUCTION FIRMS

DALLAS - Austin has been named No. 27 on Building Design + Construction's (BD+C) 2025 Top 100 Hospital Facility Construction Firms list, reaffirming the company's long-standing position as a trusted builder in the healthcare market, and highlighting continued growth in this sector.

Austin has appeared on BD+C's healthcare rankings for years, but recent performance reflects a notable expansion in both volume and opportunity. That momentum is already visible in the work underway. In 2025, Austin began preconstruction on several major healthcare projects, including JPS Health Network Hospital Addition, The University of Texas at Austin & The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Medical Complex Project (MCP), and The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Patient Care Building 1 (1PC).

This ranking is a testament to the expertise and dedication of Austin's employee-owners and highlights the importance of continued investment in people and resources as healthcare remains a key growth sector for the company.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QUANTA SERVICES, INC. [1 Press Release]

+ QUANTA SERVICES TO HOST & WEBCAST ITS 2026 INVESTOR DAY

Quanta Services, Inc. (NYSE: PWR) announced that it will host and webcast its 2026 Investor Day from New York City on Tues., Mar. 31, 2026. Management intends to present certain information and engage in a question-and-answer discussion focused on the company's strategic initiatives and new long-term financial targets. The management presentation portion of the event is scheduled to begin at approximately 9:00 a.m. Eastern time and the entire event, including the question-and-answer portion, is expected to conclude by approximately 11:30 a.m. Eastern time.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BARTON MALOW COMPANY [4 Press Releases]

+ BARTON MALOW + SHERIDAN CONSTRUCTION SET TO BUILD NEW MACON ARENA

MACON, GEORGIA - Barton Malow, in association with Sheridan Construction, has been chosen as the Construction Manager for the new Macon Arena, a major project that will replace the current Macon Coliseum and serve as a key part of the region's expanding entertainment opportunities.

Located southeast of Atlanta in Macon-Bibb County, the planned facility is designed to host various multipurpose events like minor league hockey, basketball games, high school tournaments, concerts, conferences, trade shows, and more. The new arena will offer a top-tier guest experience, featuring premium seating options like luxury suites, club suites, and loge boxes, as well as modern fan amenities such as clubs, concessions, restrooms, locker rooms, and integrated technologies. Back-of-house and support spaces will ensure the venue functions as a state-of-the-art civic facility, offering performers, event planners, and organizations a great experience.

"This arena won't be just a venue, it will be a destination and major economic engine that creates opportunities, attracts visitors, and keeps our city competitive for years to come," said Macon-Bibb Mayor Lester Miller.

As Macon's entertainment landscape continues to expand with the new Atrium Health Amphitheater and the recently renovated Macon City Auditorium, the new arena is poised to further elevate the region's growing lineup of elite venues.

"We're thrilled to partner with Sheridan Construction and bring Barton Malow's expertise to Macon-Bibb to deliver a top-tier arena. The new venue is set to become a central location in Georgia for sports, entertainment, and community gatherings," said Barton Malow Office Director Blake Boyd. "This project is an investment in Macon-Bibb County's future, and we're excited to support the local community every step of the way."

Adds Sheridan Construction President + CEO Christy Kovac. "It's a privilege for our firm to return to Macon's legacy of entertainment and civic pride, having built the original coliseum 57 years ago. Partnering with Barton Malow on this transformative new arena is both an honor and a responsibility. We believe this project will serve as a powerful economic and tourism engine, attracting visitors, creating opportunities, and enriching the lives of everyone in our community for generations to come."

Construction is planned to commence in summer 2026, with the project anticipated to reach completion in 2028.

Luke's project controls leadership role spans multiple geographic regions and consists of overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Barton Malow has announced the promotion of Luke Knochel to Vice President of Project Controls.

In his new role, Luke leads a multi‑disciplinary team responsible for core functions such as cost and schedule management, forecasting, project start‑up, accounts receivable and payable processes, and change management. His leadership spans multiple geographic regions, overseeing directors and managers across Barton Malow's operations.

Luke started at Barton Malow in 2013 as a Cost Analyst and has served in various project controls and finance leadership roles throughout his tenure. He oversaw accounts payable and accounts receivable, change management, forecasting, reporting, and more on the Little Caesars Arena project, then went on to supervise Barton Malow's offices in Chicago, Columbus, and Nashville. After a promotion to Project Controls Manager overseeing the then-Midwest business unit, Luke transitioned into a corporate finance role managing the shared service department and implementing new accounts payable software.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION U.S. CONSTRUCTION DEMAND REMAINS STRONG AMID SHIFTING MARKET CONDITIONS

Turner Construction Company announced that the First Quarter 2026 Turner Building Cost Index-which measures costs in the non-residential building construction market in the United States-increased to the value of 1530. This represents a 1.32% quarterly increase from the Fourth Quarter 2025 and a 4.87% yearly increase from the First Quarter 2025.

"Demand across the U.S. construction market remains solid, though conditions vary by region and sector. High-growth areas such as data centers, manufacturing, and semiconductor facilities are driving strong demand, particularly in the Midwest and Southeast, while traditional commercial construction has softened in several markets. At the same time, architecture and engineering activity appears to be stabilizing. Material costs such as steel, aluminum, and copper have experienced increases during the first quarter," said Attilio Rivetti, the Turner vice president responsible for compiling the Cost Index. "We are closely monitoring rising oil prices, which could begin to impact transportation and material costs in the coming quarter."

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION BREAKS GROUND ON $190 MILLION CONTRACT FOR STATE-OF-THE-ART DRINKING WATER FACILITY

DENVER, Colo. (Sept. 25, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. celebrated the groundbreaking of a $190 million contract to build the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility - an innovative project that employs advanced treatment technology to ensure clean water to residents.

The City of Westminster entrusted the PCL team to build the project's most critical components. As the lead contractor, PCL will manage the construction of cutting-edge filtration and treatment technologies. The facility will feature advanced oxidation and filtration systems designed to improve taste and odor, while enhancing resiliency against fluctuations in incoming water quality.

Designed with long-term sustainability in mind, the project replaces aging infrastructure and will provide reliable drinking water to nearly 33,000 homes and businesses. Once operational the facility will have the capacity to treat 14.7 million gallons of water per day (MGD).

"PCL is proud to celebrate the opening of the Westminster Boulevard Drinking Water Facility with the City of Westminster. This is an essential project that secures safe, reliable drinking water for the community," said Ankur Talwar, PCL's West region district manager.

The groundbreaking ceremony brought together Westminster City Council members, build partners, local leaders, and community members to celebrate the future site.

The facility is slated to be finished in 2028.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION AND PHOENIX CHILDREN'S: BUILDING A STRONGER FUTURE FOR PEDIATRIC CARE

PHOENIX, Ariz. (November 10, 2025) - PCL Construction, Inc. and Phoenix Children's are proud to celebrate a partnership dedicated to advancing pediatric health and supporting children facing critical medical challenges in Arizona.

For eight consecutive years, PCL Construction in Arizona has deepened its commitment to Phoenix Children's, increasing its annual donation to $50,000 in support of pediatric cancer care. This ongoing collaboration directly benefits children by funding medical specialists, cutting-edge technology, and family-centered care.

"Our partnership with Phoenix Children's is about more than sponsorship-it's about building hope and supporting families during their most difficult times," said Josh Yates, construction risk manager at PCL Construction. "We are honored to contribute to the hospital's mission and to help create brighter futures for children in our community."

As part of this partnership, PCL proudly sponsors Phoenix Children's annual Big Dig for Kids event. This unique experience gives pediatric patients a chance to explore construction equipment, participate in hands-on activities, and enjoy a day filled with fun and inspiration. The event not only raises vital funds for pediatric cancer care but also brings moments of joy to children and families, helping them envision a future beyond illness.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. DAVID CAMPBELL NAMED TO AZ BIG MEDIA'S 2026 "PEOPLE TO KNOW IN COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE" LIST

PHOENIX, Arizona (November 6, 2025) - PCL Buildings Inc. is pleased to announce that David Campbell, district manager for PCL Construction's Phoenix buildings office, has been named to AZ Big Media's "2026 People and Projects to Know in Commercial Real Estate" list. This annual recognition celebrates influential leaders who are shaping the future of Arizona's commercial real estate industry.

"This recognition highlights the momentum we're building in Arizona's construction market and our commitment to contributing positively to the region's future," says Campbell. "I'm proud to represent PCL and support the growth and innovation happening across Arizona."

Campbell was named as the leader of PCL's newest commercial buildings office in Phoenix this past spring, building upon a nearly 40-year legacy of excellence in water and civil construction across the Southwest. The Phoenix Buildings office marks a strategic milestone in PCL's commitment to the region, offering construction expertise across sectors including hospitality, manufacturing, aviation, and commercial and retail development.

His appearance on AZ Big Media's list reflects Campbell's contributions to the industry over the course of his nearly 20-year construction career, having led a wide range of high-profile projects across the U.S. Campbell joined PCL in 2005, with early contributions to entertainment and hospitality developments in Orlando. He later served as senior project manager on the American Dream megamall in New Jersey and held several leadership roles at PCL's U.S. head office in Denver before his current role in Phoenix.

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. RYAN HOUSE DEBUTS TEEN SUITES FOR KIDS LIVING LONGER WITH LIFE-LIMITING CONDITIONS

Ryan House, a nonprofit that provides respite and end-of-life care to children with life-limiting conditions, recently capped off its 15th anniversary year by unveiling two newly renovated teen suites. The renovations allow Ryan House to better serve children who are now surviving well into adolescence and young adulthood and were made possible through generous support from McCarthy Building Companies, which selected Ryan House as a recipient of the company's Heart Hats(TradeMark) grants, and ARC Document Solutions.

Thanks to advances in diagnostics, disease management, and comprehensive care, many children with life-limiting conditions are now living longer than once expected. Ryan House is evolving alongside the children and families it serves, making meaningful changes to better support teens through environments designed specifically for this stage of life.

The renovation was spearheaded by McCarthy's Specialized Solutions Group, which focuses on complex renovations and technical builds within healthcare and other specialized environments. The donation involved the renovation of two private suites specifically designed for teenagers and features superhero-themed decor provided by ARC that fully immerses them in a comic book-inspired atmosphere. The rooms also now include fully adjustable desks to accommodate different mobility needs, allowing teens to comfortably do schoolwork or creative activities. Each suite is equipped with the latest state-of-the-art gaming system and adaptive controllers, plus customizable LED lighting that can change colors based on each teen's preferences. Additionally, bathroom updates were made to allow for enhanced comfort and accessibility.

"This renovation speaks to how pediatric care has changed over the past decade and a half," said Dr. Wendy Bernatavicius, medical director at Ryan House and division chief of primary, complex care and adolescent medicine at Phoenix Children's Hospital. "We're seeing more adolescents and young adults with complex needs living vibrant lives. Improved medical care, early intervention and whole-family support have extended the lifespan of many of these teens. That means rethinking the environments where they're cared for. These teen suites are not just rooms. They're an affirmation of identity, belonging, and respect for each young person who calls Ryan House their home away from home."

During their recent holiday break, Ryan House welcomed two familiar guests, brothers Mason and Jaxton, who are living with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, as the first teens to stay in the newly renovated suites. While they've stayed at Ryan House before, this visit marked the first time they experienced the upgraded rooms designed specifically with teens, like them, in mind.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

urn:contentItem:6JJ7-CHH3-S44X-V24S-00000-00Your Views for May 102026-05-10T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:48:32ZLexisNexis,Your Views for May 10

Honua Ola is bad for

people and environment

In his April 30 letter to the editor, Kevin Balog states that concerns about the environmental effects of trucking wood to be burned at the proposed Honua Ola power plant at Pepe‘ekeo are overblown.

However, there are several reasons that incorporating the Honua Ola plant into the energy grid would not be in the public's interest.

Though Honua Ola might be a "renewable" energy source in the sense that additional trees could be grown to burn there, it could hardly be considered environmentally friendly. Burning wood releases more greenhouse gases than burning oil or natural gas.

In particular, emissions of methane and nitrous oxide, gases far more potent than carbon dioxide in driving climate change, are much higher from wood combustion.

Even state-of-the-art emission control systems are only marginally effective in removing these pollutants. Further, wood burning produces fine particles that increase risk of both lung and heart disease. Smokestack scrubbers can capture some of these. Others are released into the environment.

From an economic perspective, Honua Ola would charge 22 cents per kilowatt hour across a 30-year purchase agreement - 2 1/2 times the cost of energy generated by photovoltaic panels.

The state Public Utility Commission must approve any power purchase agreement involving Honua Ola before the agreement can take effect. I urge the PUC to consider both economic and environmental impacts in making their decision.

Hawaii plans to reduce greenhouse gas emissions to 50% of 2005 levels by 2030. Efforts to transition to clean energy generation and storage make sense.

Purchasing power from a greenhouse gas-generating plant does not. Richard Leman Honokaa

Stop ‘global whining'

and support Honua Ola

In response to Mr. Rusty Iijima regarding Honua Ola Bioenergy (Your views, April 24), complaining about the Trump administration and changes at the Environmental Protection Agency serves little purpose. The EPA will always be a force that Honua Ola will have to deal with.

Please see the bigger picture. Hawaii Island needs a diversified portfolio of energy producers, and Honua Ola should be part of it.

Our state faces the 2045 mandate for 100% energy production by renewable sources. For neighbor islands (Gov. Josh Green's Executive Order 25-01), the deadline is 2035! More importantly, Mr. Warren Lee, president of Honua Ola Bioenergy, pointed out that two of our island's 50-year-old fossil fuel plants are scheduled for retirement after 2028 (Your views, Feb. 15).

Those two retiring plants represent about 35 megawatts, or about 12.5%, of our island's firm generation capacity. What will fill that void? Honua Ola is capable of delivering up to 30 megawatts of firm, renewable, 24/7 power, is 99.5% complete and ready to start.

Honua Ola's inclusion to Hawaiian Electric Company's mix will increase HECO's firm, renewable production by about 30%! Honua Ola will replace fossil fuels, utilize 21st century state-of-the-art technologies to screen emissions and provide local jobs!

Of course, we could use more energy sources and bolster a diversified portfolio to meet our ever-increasing energy needs. Where are they? Where? What other large energy producer is close to 99.5% ready?

We will need Honua Ola just to maintain the energy levels we're accustomed to now.

We need solutions, not complaints. Enough with the global whining. If anybody out there wants more solar farms, wind turbines or whatever, put your money where your keyboard is and go build them. Glen Kagamida Hilo

urn:contentItem:6JJ0-D243-RTJ0-F0KT-00000-00Letters Your Voice May 92026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:48:32ZLexisNexisOPINION,OPINIONLetters Your Voice May 9

Honua Ola is bad for

people and environment

In his April 30 letter to the editor, Kevin Balog states that concerns about the environmental effects of trucking wood to be burned at the proposed Honua Ola power plant at Pepe‘ekeo are overblown.

However, there are several reasons that incorporating the Honua Ola plant into the energy grid would not be in the public's interest.

Though Honua Ola might be a "renewable" energy source in the sense that additional trees could be grown to burn there, it could hardly be considered environmentally friendly. Burning wood releases more greenhouse gases than burning oil or natural gas.

In particular, emissions of methane and nitrous oxide, gases far more potent than carbon dioxide in driving climate change, are much higher from wood combustion.

Even state-of-the-art emission control systems are only marginally effective in removing these pollutants. Further, wood burning produces fine particles that increase risk of both lung and heart disease. Smokestack scrubbers can capture some of these. Others are released into the environment.

From an economic perspective, Honua Ola would charge 22 cents per kilowatt hour across a 30-year purchase agreement - 2 1/2 times the cost of energy generated by photovoltaic panels.

The state Public Utility Commission must approve any power purchase agreement involving Honua Ola before the agreement can take effect. I urge the PUC to consider both economic and environmental impacts in making their decision.

Hawaii plans to reduce greenhouse gas emissions to 50% of 2005 levels by 2030. Efforts to transition to clean energy generation and storage make sense.

Purchasing power from a greenhouse gas-generating plant does not.

Richard Leman

Honokaa

Stop ‘global whining'

and support Honua Ola

In response to Mr. Rusty Iijima regarding Honua Ola Bioenergy (Your views, April 24), complaining about the Trump administration and changes at the Environmental Protection Agency serves little purpose. The EPA will always be a force that Honua Ola will have to deal with.

Please see the bigger picture. Hawaii Island needs a diversified portfolio of energy producers, and Honua Ola should be part of it.

Our state faces the 2045 mandate for 100% energy production by renewable sources. For neighbor islands (Gov. Josh Green's Executive Order 25-01), the deadline is 2035! More importantly, Mr. Warren Lee, president of Honua Ola Bioenergy, pointed out that two of our island's 50-year-old fossil fuel plants are scheduled for retirement after 2028 (letters to the editor, Feb. 15).

Those two retiring plants represent about 35 megawatts, or about 12.5%, of our island's firm generation capacity. What will fill that void? Honua Ola is capable of delivering up to 30 megawatts of firm, renewable, 24/7 power, is 99.5% complete and ready to start.

Honua Ola's inclusion to Hawaiian Electric Company's mix will increase HECO's firm, renewable production by about 30%! Honua Ola will replace fossil fuels, utilize 21st century state-of-the-art technologies to screen emissions and provide local jobs!

Of course, we could use more energy sources and bolster a diversified portfolio to meet our ever-increasing energy needs. Where are they? Where? What other large energy producer is close to 99.5% ready?

We will need Honua Ola just to maintain the energy levels we're accustomed to now.

We need solutions, not complaints. Enough with the global whining. If anybody out there wants more solar farms, wind turbines or whatever, put your money where your keyboard is and go build them.

Glen Kagamida

Hilo

value.Title: University of Hawaii Manoa : Historic Bachman Hall Secures LEED Gold Certification

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-8RN3-SBJ3-H52S-00000-00University of Hawaii Manoa: Historic Bachman Hall Secures LEED Gold Certification2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:48:32ZLexisNexisUniversity of Hawaii Manoa: Historic Bachman Hall Secures LEED Gold CertificationTargeted News ServiceMANOA, Hawaii

The University of Hawaii Manoa campus issued the following news release:

* * *

Historic Bachman Hall secures LEED Gold certification

The University of Hawaii at Manoa's Bachman Hall earned Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design (LEED) Gold certification on April 22, exceeding the university's standard sustainability target for major renovations. UH has the largest inventory of sustainable buildings in the state, with 28 buildings certified under the LEED program.

The certification recognizes the building's performance in energy efficiency, renewable energy generation, water conservation and indoor environmental quality following its recent modernization. University policy calls for a minimum LEED Silver certification, making Bachman Hall's rating a notable achievement for a historic campus facility.

Originally built in 1949 and designed by Hawaii architect Vladimir Ossipoff, Bachman Hall is one of the most recognizable buildings on campus. It houses administrative offices, including the Office of the President and UH Board of Regents (BOR), and features two lobby murals by artist Jean Charlot.

"We are proud to be leaders in energy and environmental designs for our campus built environments," said UH Office of Sustainability Director Miles Topping. "With this project, we have reduced our dependence on fossil fuel, conserved water, and preserved historical significance. We are committed to a sustainable future, and will continue to lead by example."

Maintaining historic character

From 2021 to 2023, the renovation maintained much of the building's original structure, reducing the need for new materials and limiting construction-related impacts. Design elements were updated to improve performance while maintaining the building's historic character.

Energy-efficient lighting and a new air conditioning system reduce energy use by about 22%. A 47-kilowatt photovoltaic (PV) system further lowered consumption, bringing the building to roughly 45% of baseline energy use. Water efficiency improvements such as low-flow plumbing fixtures reduce water use by 37%, saving an estimated 56,661 gallons annually. Permanent water meters were installed to help monitor usage and identify future conservation opportunities.

Indoor environmental quality was addressed through the use of low-emitting materials, including paints, coatings and flooring, along with improved ventilation and occupant-controlled lighting and temperature systems. Original-style jalousie windows were replaced with energy-efficient double-pane windows that include operable awnings. A reflective roof and added landscaping help reduce heat absorption, while exterior lighting was designed to minimize light pollution.

The renovation also addressed long-standing maintenance issues, including damage caused by roof leaks linked to earlier mechanical installations. The building was fully updated inside, creating flexible office space for approximately 65 employees, six conference rooms and a 2,500-square-foot boardroom used for BOR meetings.

* * *

value.Title: Letters Your Voice May 6

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHB-GGR3-RSCB-90T3-00000-00Letters Your Voice May 62026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:48:32ZLexisNexisOPINION,OPINIONLetters Your Voice May 6

High oil prices proves the

need for energy self-reliance

Hawaiian Electric's recent announcement that our electric bills will increase 20-30% due to oil price spikes caused by the Iran war is concerning. When global oil markets turn volatile, those price hikes make it harder for our families and businesses to make ends meet.

Sadly, this is nothing new. We've seen oil prices surge from global conflicts repeatedly. Each time, energy costs climb, and they seem to never fully come back down.

We, on Hawaii Island, are always hit the hardest, where our energy costs are already the highest in the entire country.

Hawaii's continued dependence on imported fossil fuels leaves us exposed to global forces far beyond our control. Which is why the situation with Honua Ola Bioenergy needs to be resolved.

This state‑of‑the‑art, renewable energy facility in Pepe‘ekeo can supply up to 20% of Hawaii Island's power with 24-hours-a-day, seven-days-a-week firm energy, sourced from locally grown eucalyptus crops and cleared invasive species like albizia - instead of relying on foreign oil.

Yet, instead of generating power, the plant sits idle, caught in delays, even while there is an agreement in principle with Hawaiian Electric. At a time when oil volatility is threatening our economic stability, we have a 99% completed, fully permitted, renewable energy facility that would be a part of Hawaii Island's energy solution.

Experts estimate that Honua Ola could replace one-third to one-half of the island's fossil fuel dependence, and at set prices for the next 30 years that won't be subjected to sharp increases in world oil prices.

Every delay in renewable energy projects like Honua Ola, or new technologies like green hydrogen, keeps our island vulnerable. And every electric bill is a reminder that our dependence on fossil fuels is not sustainable – economically or environmentally.

If we want to break this cycle once and for all, we must make energy self‑reliance a real priority and take action.

Jerry Chang

State representative, 1989-2012

Hilo

Trump's opposition to

wind farms makes no sense

After reading up on wind farms, I cannot understand why President Trump wants to cancel the building of them on our coasts. It seem that having natural energy can only benefit the people.

Why would he pay to have them not build these wind farms when it can only help the people and lessen the need for fossil fuels?

What am I missing here?

Colleen Wallis

Kailua-Kona

value.Title: Per Capita: Poking holes in the planet

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHD-T233-RX41-Y2MX-00000-00Per Capita: Poking holes in the planet2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:48:32ZLexisNexisFEATURESPer Capita: Poking holes in the planetJohn MottSmith

I was in grade school when I first heard the word "extraction." It was used when people asked where your ancestors came from. "What's your ethnic background?"

My family came mostly from England, but some via Hawaii. Dentists also used it when "extracting" a tooth.

Nowadays, while these definitions may still have currency, the term, at least as it enters my consciousness, relates to what we humans are taking out of the ground and oceans, primarily for the energy, either directly or indirectly, we need to power our homes, industries, transportation, essentially the whole shebang of our lifestyles.

As far as I can tell, there's no source of power not coal, oil, gas, solar, wind or nuclear that does not involve some level of extraction. But there are big differences in the degree of how much extraction is required and what mess each one makes.

Generally speaking, coal is mined in deep tunnels into the earth or, if close to the surface, strip mined or extracted from open pits. Getting coal to where it can be burned to produce energy requires trains. Each bit of coal can only be burned once, so the tunnels get deeper and the pits get larger. Burning coal has been a major cause of increased CO2 emissions into the atmosphere.

Oil and gas are also brought to the surface, in this case by wells drilled into the earth, either on land or in the ocean. On shore wells are typically from 3,000 to 35,000 feet deep, ocean wells can be much deeper. The Deepwater Horizon oil spill from its 35,000-foot well resulted in the release of 200 million gallons into the Gulf of Mexico.

Fracking, using a huge amount of water and other liquids to break up subsurface rock formations, provides further access to oil and gas.

Solar, wind and nuclear energy also require some extraction of rare-earth elements for items such as solar arrays and wind turbines. Interestingly, according to Wikipedia, the term "rare earths" is a misnomer, since they are generally found not as pure metals but in compounds that have to be processed from raw ore, which is an energy-intensive process.

While most of the holes poked in the planet are to extract oil and gas, the global move towards renewable energy technologies, such as electric vehicles, wind turbines and batteries, along with advanced electronics, so-called defense applications, and consumer products such as smart phones has increased demand for rare earths.

And because the rare-earth elements are so diffuse, people are always looking for areas where the concentration is enough to make it economical to mine them. Here's a couple of such locations in the recent news.

* The ocean. Recent New York Times articles describe very serious efforts underway to mine the ocean floor.

"Parts of the ocean floor are covered with fist-sized nodules that contain nickel, cobalt, manganese and copper." Mining the ocean floor is not yet widespread, in part due to a United Nations organization, the International Seabed Authority, that has been for decades "trying to negotiate rules to regulate mining in international waters."

Not surprisingly, the U.S. is not a member of that effort. Also unsurprising, participating countries have not been able to agree on regulations. Really, really not surprising, President Trump has made seabed mining a priority and signed one of his thousands of executive orders to get things going, not just in territorial waters, but anywhere in the open ocean.

One business, The Metals Company, has conducted tests and says it is ready to go whole-hog in 2027. Japan has run a test program in its territorial waters, vacuuming subsurface mud from a depth of 6,000 feet. Mining the oceans is at the starting gate and, without regulations, the head of the International Seabed Authority fears a Wild West where each country make up its own rules, if any.

* Antarctica. Mining is currently banned on this continent, but people are thinking ahead. The Times reports that "a new study finds that as the climate continues to heat up over the next decades tens of thousands of square miles of Antarctica will lose their protective covering of ice, exposing valuable deposits of copper, iron, gold, silver, platinum and cobalt."

* California. No kidding. According to another Times article, "Beneath California's Salton Sea, there is so much metal essential to rechargeable batteries that Gov. Gavin Newsom calls the vast lake the Saudia Arabia of Lithium."

* The moon. There's serious interest in mining the moon, and, as we have witnessed, there's increasing interest in going there, this time by private companies as well as governments.

Some may think of the moon as a gas station on the road to Mars but others, such as Rob Meyerson, founder of Interlune, a company that is focused on mining Helium-3, a gas made inside the sun, only present in trace amounts on Earth, but deposited on the moon's surface over billions of years by the solar wind.

According to Meyerson "this could become vital for quantum computers and, theoretically, nuclear fusion." Further, 'While the demand for Helium-3 is growing, supply is extremely limited. It's a product that is priced high enough to warrant going to space to bring it back to Earth."

John Mott-Smith is a resident of Davis. This column appears the first and third Wednesday of each month in the Davis Enterprise. Please send comments to johnmottsmith@comcast.net

value.Title: Ameresco 's Kupono Project Named Winner in 2026 Environment+Energy Leader Awards

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JM5-K8X3-RYN4-F4M3-00000-00Ameresco's Kupono Project Named Winner in 2026 Environment+Energy Leader Awards2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:48:32ZLexisNexisAmeresco's Kupono Project Named Winner in 2026 Environment+Energy Leader Awards

Key Highlights:

* Project reduces 50,000 tons of CO annually, equivalent to taking 12,000 cars off the road each year.

* Project supports local labor, STEM education, and community organizations with philanthropic investments.

* Innovative land stewardship ensures minimal environmental disruption during construction and operations.

Original Press Release:

FRAMINGHAM, Massachusetts, May 5 -- Ameresco Inc. issued the following news release:

- Public-private partnership with the U.S. Navy and Hawaiian Electric delivers renewable energy, grid resilience, and community benefits on Oahu

Ameresco, Inc., (NYSE: AMRC), a leading energy infrastructure solutions provider, today announced that its Kupono Project in Hawai'i has been named a winner in the 2026 Environment+Energy Leader Awards. The annual program recognizes companies delivering measurable progress in energy management, environmental performance, and sustainability.

Award-Winning Solar and Storage Innovation in Hawai'i

Honored in the Environmental Impact category for reducing emissions, strengthening grid resilience, and expanding access to reliable renewable energy for underserved communities on Oahu, the Kupono Project was one of this year's standout entries across categories spanning product innovation, project implementation, startup advancement, and organizational leadership.

E+E Leader Awards 2026 Winner Logo

The project pairs a 42 MW solar array with a 42 MW/168 MWh lithium-ion battery energy storage system, delivering reliable power for approximately 10,000 homes. Structured as a 20-year public-private partnership with the U.S. Navy and Hawaiian Electric (HECO), Kupono reduces more than 50,000 tons of CO annually, equivalent to taking 12,000 cars off the road each year, and supports Hawaii's statutory goal of 100% renewable energy generation and carbon neutrality by 2045.

Beyond environmental performance, the project also demonstrates how large-scale infrastructure investment can deliver meaningful community benefits. Built with local labor and materials, the project supports Hawai'i's growing renewable energy workforce and funds philanthropic investments in STEM education, youth sports, and community organizations across the island. Thoughtful land stewardship, including the safe relocation of 500,000 bees and ongoing vegetation management by a herd of 200 sheep, ensured minimal disruption to the surrounding environment throughout construction and ongoing operations.

A Testament to Excellence

Recognition from Environment+Energy Leader highlights the Kupono Project as a standout example of innovation and performance in today's evolving energy and sustainability landscape. Judges highlighted the project's strong public-private partnership structure, innovative land stewardship, and its "excellent alignment with state and federal decarbonization targets."

"As organizations navigate an increasingly dynamic and uncertain operating environment, the ability to improve efficiency, reduce emissions, and deliver measurable results has never been more critical," said Sarah Roberts, Co-President and Publisher of Environment+Energy Leader. "This year's winners demonstrate the innovation and leadership required to move forward with clarity and impact."

For Ameresco, the recognition reflects the power of collaboration and a long-standing commitment to building resilient energy infrastructure.

"The Kupono Project reflects exactly what is possible when public, private, and community stakeholders come together around a shared purpose," said Nicole Bulgarino, Co-President of Ameresco. "This recognition reflects the strong collaboration with the U.S. Navy and Hawaiian Electric, the local teams who built this project, and the communities in Hawai'i who will benefit from reliable renewable energy for decades to come."

About Ameresco, Inc.

Founded in 2000, Ameresco, Inc. (NYSE:AMRC) is a leading energy infrastructure solutions provider dedicated to helping customers reduce costs, enhance resilience, and decarbonize to net zero in the global energy transition. Our comprehensive portfolio includes implementing smart energy efficiency solutions, upgrading aging infrastructure, and developing, constructing, and operating distributed energy resources. As a trusted full-service partner, Ameresco shows the way by reducing energy use and delivering energy infrastructure solutions to Federal, state and local governments, utilities, data centers, educational and healthcare institutions, housing authorities, and commercial and industrial customers. Headquartered in Framingham, MA, Ameresco has more than 1,500 employees providing local expertise in North America and Europe. For more information, visit www.ameresco.com.

About the Environment + Energy Leader Awards

Now in its 14th year, the Environment+Energy Leader Awards program recognizes excellence across products, projects, startups and organizational initiatives that deliver meaningful advancements in environmental programs, sustainability, and energy management. Entries are evaluated by an independent panel of industry experts, with a focus on innovation, scalability, and measurable impact. Winners are recognized as leaders in advancing best practices and setting new standards across the global energy and environmental landscape.

[Category: Renewable Energy, Energy, Partnerships, Award Wins, ESG and Sustainability, Sustainability Initiatives, Innovation]

Source: Ameresco Inc.

value.Title: SWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT NEWS INCLUDING PEER NEWS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGX-W7J3-SG48-B1KR-00000-00SWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT NEWS INCLUDING PEER NEWS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:38:45ZLexisNexisSTOCKSWINERTON INCORPORATED - THE RECENT NEWS INCLUDING PEER NEWS

DASHBOARD: SWINERTON INCORPORATED

PermID4296594671Websitehttp://www.swinerton.comIndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress5707 SOUTHWEST PARKWAY, BLDG. 1, SUITE 200 | AUSTIN, TX 78735

ACTIVITIES:

Swinerton Incorporated provides commercial construction and construction management services in the Western United States. It offers general construction, modernization and renovation, management and consulting, preconstruction, corporate, and renewable energy services. The companys projects include aviation, cultural, education, federal, gaming and entertainment, healthcare, hospitality, interiors, mission critical, mixed-use/residential, office, renewable energy, retail, and special. Swinerton Incorporated was formerly known as Swinerton & Walberg Co. The company was founded in 1888 and is based in San Francisco, California with additional offices in California, Colorado, Hawaii, Texas, New Mexico, and Washington.

+ SWINERTON INCORPORATED [3 News That Matters]

+ SWINERTON STRENGTHENS MISSION CRITICAL TEAM WITH KRISTEN BEJARANO APPOINTMENT

Swinerton Incorporated has announced the appointment of Kristen Bejarano as Director of Mission Critical. This strategic move, revealed on the 8th of April, 2026, underscores Swinerton's commitment to enhancing its capabilities in the burgeoning mission critical and data center infrastructure market.Bejarano brings nearly 25 years of extensive experience in delivering complex, high-demand projects, having made significant contributions to the mission critical construction landscape for some of the largest global technology firms. Her notable achievements include launching and scaling a national mission critical and data center construction division, and managing the strategy and procurement for a major technology company's Americas data center program.In her new role at Swinerton, Bejarano will spearhead the company's mission critical strategy, collaborating with clients to translate intricate technical requirements into actionable plans that emphasize speed-to-market, sustainability, and energy efficiency. Chris Chany, Chief Revenue Officer of Swinerton, expressed confidence in Bejarano's ability to significantly enhance the growth of the mission critical sector while also improving client experiences.Bejarano's passion for the evolving landscape of mission critical engineering, particularly in areas such as cooling technologies and renewable energy integration, positions her as a key leader in delivering innovative solutions. Her leadership is expected to enable Swinerton to address the growing demands of the market while maintaining a strong focus on sustainability and operational efficiency.Swinerton Incorporated, founded in 1888 and wholly employee-owned, comprises Swinerton Builders, Timberlab, Inc., SAK Builders, Inc., and Lindgren Development Inc. It provides a range of construction services across the United States, with a commitment to quality and innovation in every project.

+ SWINERTON STRENGTHENS NORTHEAST DIVISION WITH NEW LEADERSHIP

Swinerton Builders, an industry-leading commercial general contractor with offices nationwide, has announced a significant leadership transition in its Northeast Division. This promotion of Jay Quackenbush to vice president and division manager is part of a broader strategy to bolster the firm’s presence in the growing Northeast construction market. Following this move, which took effect on 16th March 2026, the Northeast Division has also welcomed Nicholas Schifferle as director of client relations and Robert Murphy as project executive.Jay Quackenbush, a long-time New York native, brings nearly three decades of experience overseeing complex construction projects to his new role. His deep understanding of New York City's unique building conditions and extensive industry relationships position him well to drive the division's strategic goals. Ray Haj, Swinerton's chief operational officer and East Coast regional manager, emphasized that Quackenbush's leadership will enhance the team's capacity to meet client needs, ranging from facility management to large-scale ground-up projects. Furthermore, Quackenbush is actively involved in community engagement, serving on several boards and partnering with local nonprofits.Nicholas Schifferle, who joined Swinerton in 2025, adds more than 20 years of international business development experience to the team. His background includes managing high-profile design and construction projects across Asia and Australia, as well as founding a design-build firm in San Francisco. Quackenbush noted Schifferle's ability to adapt to rapid industry changes and his people-focused approach, which has made him a valuable asset to both the team and its clients. Schifferle's longstanding involvement with CoreNet Global reflects his commitment to advancing the commercial real estate sector.Meanwhile, Robert Murphy, with his extensive portfolio of overseeing over $650 million in commercial interiors construction, is tasked with leading multiple project teams to ensure quality and client satisfaction. Quackenbush praised Murphy's operational capabilities and his ability to elevate team performance. Together with long-standing division leaders Sloane Brown and Peter Fontana, Murphy will work to grow Swinerton’s footprint in the Northeast's commercial interiors market.Since its establishment in the Northeast in 2021, Swinerton's division has delivered a variety of significant projects, including office fit-outs, historic renovations, and ground-up constructions across all five boroughs. With a robust project pipeline and a commitment to excellence, the team continues to expand its portfolio into education, residential, and public sectors. Quackenbush affirmed that the division is well-positioned for future growth, driven by a talented team dedicated to serving the communities in which they operate.

+ SWINERTON ANNOUNCES LEADERSHIP TRANSITION AS DON ADAIR RETIRES

Swinerton Incorporated has announced that Executive Vice President and Chief Revenue Officer (CRO) Don Adair will retire on January 9, 2026, after nearly four decades in the construction industry. This leadership change comes as the company prepares for continued growth and strategic development under new leadership.Adair's retirement was officially communicated to the employee-owners of Swinerton on 13th June 2025, marking the conclusion of a notable career that began in 1988 when he joined Swinerton Builders as a Project Engineer in Los Angeles. Throughout his tenure, Adair held various pivotal roles, including expanding Swinerton Builders' San Diego office and ultimately being named Executive Vice President and CRO in 2019.In his retirement announcement, Adair expressed pride in the company's culture, emphasizing the importance of building strong teams and lasting relationships. 'Watching those efforts grow into long-term success for both our teams and our partners has been one of the most rewarding parts of my journey,' he stated.Chris Chany, who has collaborated with Adair for nearly a decade, has been appointed as his successor. Chany joined Swinerton Builders in 2013 and has since become known for his ability to foster partnerships and drive strategic growth. His focus on delivering extraordinary client experiences and reliable project execution positions him as a strong candidate to continue the momentum established by Adair.In his new role, Chany emphasized a commitment to maintaining the company's core values while pursuing new opportunities for growth. 'I am honored to step into this role and continue building on Don's framework,' Chany remarked. 'Our focus will remain on building strong relationships and driving growth for our teams and clients.' With Chany at the helm, Swinerton aims to enhance its reputation as a leading player in the construction sector.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BALFOUR BEATTY [3 News That Matters]

+ BALFOUR BEATTY ELEVATES TRENT JOHNSON TO LEAD AVIATION OPERATIONS IN THE CAROLINAS

Balfour Beatty has promoted Trent Johnson to Vice President of operations in the Carolinas, effective immediately. Johnson's new role will see him leading and expanding the company’s aviation sector across North and South Carolina, building on a career that spans over 19 years in the construction industry.As of 1st April 2026, Trent Johnson’s promotion is a reflection of his extensive experience, including more than 12 and a half years with Balfour Beatty. Chapman expressed excitement over the project’s complexity, stating that JMA Ventures is drawn to intricate developments, which informed their choice in partners like Turner Construction.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ENTERPRISES, INC. [3 News That Matters]

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION SECURES SPOT ON FORTUNE 100 BEST COMPANIES TO WORK FOR

PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. has been recognized as one of the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For in 2026. This acknowledgment comes as a testament to PCL's commitment to fostering a high-trust workplace environment, reflecting the company's dedication to employee satisfaction and engagement. The official announcement was made on 2nd April 2026.This marks the 17th time PCL Construction has earned a place on this prestigious list, achieving a notable rank of 91 this year. The recognition highlights PCL's sustained efforts in creating a supportive and empowering work environment, where employees feel valued and motivated to contribute to the company's success.Deron Brown, president and chief operating officer of PCL's U.S. operations, emphasized that the company's employee-owned structure plays a critical role in shaping its culture. He noted, 'Our people are directly invested in shaping a culture where they feel trusted, supported and excited about the future.' This approach not only enhances employee morale but also drives the overall performance of PCL Construction.PCL's recognition comes on the heels of a successful year, during which the company also ranked No. 11 on Engineering News-Record’s Top 400 Contractors list for 2025. This accolade underscores PCL Construction's leadership in executing complex projects across North America while maintaining its focus on employee well-being and satisfaction.The Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For list serves as a benchmark for organizational health and employee experience. Companies like PCL Construction, which consistently rank high on this list, often see a positive correlation between employee trust and business performance, reinforcing the belief that a supportive workplace is essential for long-term success.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION TACKLES COLORADO RIVER CRISIS WITH INNOVATIVE WATER SOLUTIONS

As the Colorado River faces unprecedented challenges, PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. is stepping up to provide innovative solutions aimed at water resilience. On the 26th of March 2026, the urgency surrounding the Colorado River's dwindling water supply became a focal point for communities across the Western United States, as they seek alternatives to secure their water futures.The Colorado River serves as a vital resource for millions, yet it is currently grappling with severe drought conditions. Lake Powell, for instance, is at an alarming 162 feet below full capacity, holding only 26% of its total water volume. The U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that if current conditions persist, Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a critical reassessment of water management strategies across the basin states.PCL Construction is at the forefront of this reassessment, advocating for investments in water reuse infrastructure as a key strategy for bolstering water security. With projected U.S. investments in water reuse expected to average $47.1 billion over the next decade, PCL is positioned to support municipalities in capturing and reusing water to mitigate the effects of ongoing drought. Although adoption rates vary significantly among the seven Colorado River basin states, PCL's initiatives exemplify how infrastructure investments can enhance resilience in water supply systems.The urgency of the situation is underscored by the imminent expiration of current water management rules in the fall of 2026. As states grapple with the looming deadline to ratify Colorado River water allocation agreements, PCL Construction's projects, such as the Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix, provide tangible examples of how communities can adapt to a drier future. This program has been crucial in ensuring the reliability of water supply for the rapidly growing Phoenix area, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade.Industry leaders, including PCL, recently gathered at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference to discuss collaborative strategies for securing the river's future. The consensus was clear: a multifaceted approach is necessary to address the complexities of climate change and aging infrastructure. PCL Construction's commitment to advancing water reuse initiatives positions the company as a critical player in shaping sustainable water management practices that can withstand the challenges posed by the Colorado River's ongoing crisis.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ADVANCES $192M AI-READY DATA CENTER PROJECT

PCL Construction Services, Inc. and PCL Industrial Services, Inc., operating companies of PCL Construction, have reached structural completion on a $192 million, 22.5-megawatt data center, advancing one of the largest digital infrastructure projects currently underway in the Denver metropolitan area. On 19th March 2026, the companies celebrated this significant milestone that underscores their commitment to delivering state-of-the-art facilities designed for high-capacity digital workloads.Located on a 17-acre site southeast of Denver, the project is being delivered by PCL’s Minneapolis Buildings group in collaboration with the Denver team, combining longstanding client relationships with local market expertise. The data center is specifically designed to support enterprise-scale, compute-intensive workloads, including artificial intelligence and graphics processing unit applications, as demand for high-capacity digital infrastructure continues to grow across the region.PCL’s scope of work includes the phased construction of five steel-frame colocation data halls totaling approximately 277,000 square feet, allowing for efficient onboarding of new tenants while maintaining flexibility for future expansion. The project also features a dedicated administration building with office space for personnel and on-site operations teams. This design aims to accommodate high-density deployments, aligning performance needs with modern sustainability and energy-efficiency requirements.The project includes comprehensive electrical infrastructure to support continuous, high-performance operations, from permanent and temporary power systems to sitewide distribution, security, and communications pathways. Key work includes installing and integrating major power and cooling equipment that enables reliable, high-density data center operations. PCL Industrial Services, Inc. is self-performing the core electrical work while coordinating specialized partners for testing and life-safety systems.PCL Construction’s extensive experience in delivering mission-critical and data center facilities across the United States is critical on complex, high-density projects like this, according to Ryan Schmidt, vice president and district manager of Denver Buildings. The project teams recently marked the topping-out ceremony, celebrating the dedication, craftsmanship, and collaboration that will transition the development into interior build-out and systems installation, with construction scheduled for completion in summer 2027.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION UNVEILS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ENHANCE MANUFACTURING CAPABILITIES

JE Dunn Construction has launched its new off-site manufacturing initiative, Form Off-Site Solutions(TradeMark), designed to specialize in Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. This strategic move comes as the company seeks to capitalize on its century-long legacy in Kansas City and beyond. The introduction of Form Off-Site Solutions is positioned as a response to the growing demand for advanced manufacturing capabilities, especially in a rapidly evolving construction landscape. The launch date of this initiative is 6th April 2026.Led by Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim, Form Off-Site Solutions brings together skilled craftspeople, advanced automation, and innovative strategies to design and produce specialized components for a diverse array of stakeholders, including owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers. The offerings are categorized into four distinct product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, ensuring that various market needs are met with tailored solutions.The end-to-end service model of Form Off-Site Solutions addresses the multifaceted challenges inherent in construction projects. By providing capabilities that range from estimating and design to manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, JE Dunn Construction aims to deliver improved installation efficiency, enhanced project safety and quality, and maximized cost certainty. This comprehensive approach is intended to alleviate labor shortages and accelerate project timelines, critical factors in today's construction environment.Effenheim emphasizes the collaborative nature of Form's operations, stating, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart." This commitment to collaboration and innovation is echoed by JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford, who notes that the development of Form Off-Site Solutions represents a significant advancement in the company's ongoing quest to drive innovation for its clients.With production facilities located in Kansas City and Nashville, Form Off-Site Solutions is poised to serve clients on a national scale. The company's focus on innovation and strategic thinking is a cornerstone of its operations, aimed at enhancing project safety and quality while also addressing labor shortages in the industry. As JE Dunn Construction continues to push the boundaries of off-site manufacturing, the launch of Form Off-Site Solutions marks a pivotal step in its mission to deliver projects with greater efficiency and certainty.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION CELEBRATES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE FOR WORKFORCE CENTER

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — Construction has reached a key milestone on the Northland Workforce Development Center, as JE Dunn Construction and project partners recently celebrated the topping out of the new facility. The event, held on 19th March 2026, marked the placement of the final structural steel beam for the project, signaling significant progress on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story building designed to expand access to career and technical education across the Northland region.Prior to the beam’s installation, attendees participated in a traditional beam-signing ceremony, adding their names and messages to a steel beam bearing project and partner logos. This moment underscored the collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner school districts, project stakeholders, and the broader community in advancing a shared workforce development vision. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, emphasized the importance of this collaboration, stating, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The Northland Workforce Development Center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services. Once complete, the facility will expand the capacity of Northland Career Center and its partners, serving both high school students and adult learners seeking industry-aligned training and credentials. Planned program spaces include flexible labs and classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplace conditions.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated following the structural milestone. Brian Noller, Executive Director of Business Services and NWDC Campaign Lead at Platte County School District, highlighted the collaborative efforts, stating, 'With support from the State of Missouri, local municipalities, businesses and industries, individual investors, and community foundations, we are making a long-term investment in the success of our students and the future of our region.'JE Dunn is serving as construction manager for the project, working in partnership with the school district and design firm Clark & Enersen. The project team has emphasized safety, schedule adherence, and coordination throughout construction, while maintaining communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities. The Northland Workforce Development Center represents a regional investment in workforce readiness, aligning educational infrastructure with evolving industry needs and supporting long-term economic development in the Kansas City area.

+ CHRIS CHELETTE REJOINS JE DUNN TO BOLSTER HEALTHCARE SECTOR

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025.During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region.In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction.Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively.As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.
